name: Multi-dimensional competitive analysis framework
description: 12 dimensions (runtime, security, multi-tenancy, chat, tools, memory, LLM, self-evolution, infra, DX, enterprise, performance) with 89 sub-features. 0-5 scoring scale based on implemented features. Evidence from code inspection, docs, releases, community. Reveals market positioning gaps and strategic priorities.

title: Chimera market positioning: Agent-as-a-Service vs self-hosted frameworks
rationale: OpenClaw/NemoClaw/OpenFang are all self-hosted personal assistant frameworks targeting developers who want to run agents on their own hardware. Chimera is Agent-as-a-Service targeting enterprises who want agents-as-infrastructure without managing the platform. This is not direct competition but different market segments. Chimera's moat: multi-tenancy + self-evolution + AWS-native. Critical gaps: no community marketplace (vs ClawHub), cold start performance (2-3s vs 180ms), autonomous agents (vs OpenFang Hands).
